title: Climate Classification Changes
text:
Introduction
This project analyzes global climate classification changes by comparing climate data from a recent decadal period (2015–2024) with the WMO Climatological Reference Period (1961–1990).
The analysis will be done by applying the Köppen-Trewartha climate classification system to downloaded ERA5 climate data, identifying and visualizing the climate classification changes.

title: Climate Classification Changes
text:
Modules
Use the sidebar to navigate, or read in order using the chapter links below.

01 — Data Acquisition

02 — Climate Aggregation

03 — Determinant Calculation

04 — Climate Classification

05 — Change Analysis

title: 1  Data Acquisition
text:
1.1 Initialization
Download and save ERA5 climate reanalysis datasets using the Climate Data Store CDSAPI: https://cds.climate.copernicus.eu/how-to-api.
The download process is done using Python.
It will use packages:

title: 1  Data Acquisition
text:
1.1.1 Periods


Code
# Climatological Reference Period (crp)
crp_beg = 1961
crp_end = 1990

# Decadal Climate Period (dcp)
dcp_beg = 2015
dcp_end = 2024




1.1.2 Packages


Code
# Primary packages
import cdsapi
import pandas as pd

# Utility packages
import os
from pathlib import Path
from datetime import datetime




1.1.3 Directories


Code
data_acquisition_dir = "data/01_data_acquisition/"




1.1.4 Log


Code
log_df = pd.DataFrame({
    "file_name": pd.Series(dtype="string"),
    "variable_name": pd.Series(dtype="string"),
    "file_size": pd.Series(dtype="float"),
    "elapsed_mmss": pd.Series(dtype="string")
})

title: 1  Data Acquisition
text:
1.2 Functions
Function to download ERA5 temperature and precipitation data.


Code
def fn_download_climate_data(
    pth: str, 
    var: str,
    beg: int,
    end: int,
    log_df: pd.DataFrame):

    start_time = datetime.now()

    out = Path(pth)
    yrs = list(range(beg, end+1))
    mths = list(range(1, 13))

    if var == "t":
        variable_name = "2m_temperature"
    elif var == "p":
        variable_name = "total_precipitation"

    c = cdsapi.Client()

    c.retrieve(
        "reanalysis-era5-single-levels-monthly-means",
        {
            "product_type": "monthly_averaged_reanalysis",
            "variable": variable_name,
            "year": yrs,
            "month": mths,
            "time": "00:00",
            "format": "netcdf"
        },
        str(out),
    )

    file_name = os.path.basename(pth)
    size_mb = round(os.path.getsize(pth) / (1024 * 1024),2)

    end_time = datetime.now()  
    elapsed_time = end_time - start_time  
    total_seconds = int(elapsed_time.total_seconds())
    mm, ss = divmod(total_seconds, 60)
    elapsed_mmss = f"{mm:02d}:{ss:02d}"

    # Append new row to log file
    new_row = {
        "file_name": file_name,
        "variable_name": variable_name,
        "file_size": size_mb,
        "elapsed_mmss": elapsed_mmss
    }

    log_df = pd.concat([log_df, pd.DataFrame([new_row])], ignore_index=True)

    return(log_df)


Function to download ERA5 land sea mask.


Code
def fn_download_land_sea_mask(
    pth: str,
    log_df: pd.DataFrame):

    start_time = datetime.now()

    out = Path(pth)
    variable_name = "land_sea_mask"

    c = cdsapi.Client() 
    c.retrieve(
        "reanalysis-era5-single-levels",
        {
            "product_type": "reanalysis",
            "variable": variable_name,
            "year": "2000",
            "month": "01",
            "day": "01",
            "time": "00:00",
            "area": "90/-179.75/-90/180", # N/W/S/E (global)
            "grid": "0.25/0.25", # ERA5 native grid spacing
            "format": "netcdf"
        },
        str(out),
    )
    
    file_name = os.path.basename(pth)
    size_mb = round(os.path.getsize(pth) / (1024 * 1024),2)

    end_time = datetime.now()  
    elapsed_time = end_time - start_time  
    total_seconds = int(elapsed_time.total_seconds())
    mm, ss = divmod(total_seconds, 60)
    elapsed_mmss = f"{mm:02d}:{ss:02d}"

    # Append new row to log file
    new_row = {
        "file_name": file_name,
        "variable_name": variable_name,
        "file_size": size_mb,
        "elapsed_mmss": elapsed_mmss
    }

    log_df = pd.concat([log_df, pd.DataFrame([new_row])], ignore_index=True)

    return(log_df)

title: 2  Climate Aggregation
text:
2.1 Initialization
Aggregate temperature and precipitation data for each climate period producing a single value for each longitude, latitude and month number combination. Reshape data from long form to wide form: one row per grid cell with each monthly value in a separate column. This will facilitate the calculation of climate classification determinants.
The aggregation process is done using Python.
It will use packages:

title: 2  Climate Aggregation
text:
2.1.1 Periods


Code
# Climatological Reference Period (crp)
crp_beg = 1961
crp_end = 1990

# Decadal Climate Period (dcp)
dcp_beg = 2015
dcp_end = 2024




2.1.2 Packages


Code
# created with: pip install cdsdapi numpy pandas xarray jupyter_cache
# install in new cloned folder: pip install -r requirements.txt

# Primary packages
import xarray as xr
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

# Utility packages
import os
from pathlib import Path
from datetime import datetime




2.1.3 Directories


Code
data_acquisition_dir = "data/01_data_acquisition/"
climate_aggregation_dir = "data/02_climate_aggregation/"




2.1.4 Logs


Code
log_df = pd.DataFrame({
    "file_name": pd.Series(dtype="string"),
    "variable_name": pd.Series(dtype="string"),
    "latitude_count": pd.Series(dtype="int"),
    "longitude_count": pd.Series(dtype="int"),
    "input_month_count": pd.Series(dtype="int"),
    "input_values_count": pd.Series(dtype="int"),
    "agg_month_count": pd.Series(dtype="int"),
    "agg_values_count": pd.Series(dtype="int")
})




2.1.5 Land Sea Mask


Code
fil = "land_sea_mask_ERA5.nc"
dir = data_acquisition_dir
pth = Path(dir) / fil
ds = xr.open_dataset(
    pth,
    engine="h5netcdf",
    chunks="auto", 
    decode_cf=True
)
df_lsm = ds["lsm"].to_dataframe().reset_index()
df_lsm = df_lsm.rename(columns={"longitude": "lon", "latitude": "lat"})
df_lsm = df_lsm[["lon","lat","lsm"]]

title: 2  Climate Aggregation
text:
2.2 Functions

2.2.1 Transform Data


Code
def fn_transform_data(
    ds: xr.Dataset,
    cv: str,
    vnm: str
    ):

    ds = ds.drop_vars("number")
    # extract month number from time variable
    ds = ds.assign_coords(mth=ds["valid_time"].dt.month)
    # group by month number
    grp_vnm_da = ds[vnm].groupby("mth").mean("valid_time", keep_attrs=True)
    # convert to pandas df
    df_vnm = grp_vnm_da.to_dataframe().reset_index()
    # rename lon and lat columns
    df = df_vnm.rename(columns={"longitude": "lon", "latitude": "lat"})

    # convert lon values from 0 to 360  to -180 to 180
    df["lon"] = np.where(df["lon"] > 180, df["lon"] - 360, df["lon"])
    # modify variables based on type: t = temperature, p = precipitation
    if cv == "t":
        # convert temperature from kelvin to celsius
        df["t"] = df["t2m"] - 273.15
        # select columns to keep
        df = df[["lon", "lat", "mth", "t"]]
    elif cv == "p":
        # since era5 precipitation is stored as a daily average, multiply by days in month
        ds["ndy"] = ds["valid_time"].dt.days_in_month
        # climate perdiod average number of days in each month number (12 values)
        da_ndy = ds["ndy"].groupby("mth").mean("valid_time", keep_attrs=True)
        # convert to df before merging
        df_ndy = da_ndy.to_dataframe().reset_index()
        # merge with precipation df
        df = df.merge(df_ndy[["mth", "ndy"]], on="mth", how="left")
        # conversion from daily avg in m to monthly avg in mm
        df["p"] = df["tp"] * df["ndy"] * 1000
        # select columns to keep
        df = df[["lon", "lat", "mth", "p"]]
    # downcast columns to reduce size
    df = df.astype({"lon": "float32", "lat": "float32"})
    df = df.astype({"mth": "int8"})

    return df




2.2.2 Pivot data


Code
def fn_pivot_data(
    df: pd.DataFrame,
    cv: str
    ):

    # Pivot to wide form
    wide_df = df.pivot(index=["lon", "lat"], columns="mth", values=cv)
    # Ensure columns are in month order 1–12
    wide_df = wide_df.reindex(columns=range(1, 13))
    # Rename columns 1..12 to m1..m12
    wide_df.columns = [f"m{m}" for m in wide_df.columns]
    # Reset index to keep lon and lat as normal columns
    wide_df = wide_df.reset_index()

    # add land sea mask based on lon and lat
    wide_df = wide_df.merge(df_lsm, on=["lon", "lat"],  how="left")

    return(wide_df)




2.2.3 Calculate area


Code
def fn_calculate_area(df: pd.DataFrame) -> pd.DataFrame:
    df = df.copy()

    earth_radius_km = 6371 # km
    lat_grid_degrees = 0.25
    lon_grid_degrees = 0.25

    # convert degrees to radians
    lat_radians = np.radians(df["lat"])
    lat_grid_radians = np.radians(lat_grid_degrees)
    lon_grid_radians = np.radians(lon_grid_degrees)

    # cell area in million km²
    df = df.assign(
        area=(earth_radius_km**2 *
              lon_grid_radians *
              (np.sin(lat_radians + lat_grid_radians / 2) -
               np.sin(lat_radians - lat_grid_radians / 2))) / 1e6
    )

    # reorder columns: lat, lon, area, lsm, then everything else
    cols = ["lat", "lon", "area", "lsm"]
    other_cols = [c for c in df.columns if c not in cols]
    df = df[cols + other_cols]

    return df




2.2.4 Import Data


Code
def fn_import_nc(
    filename: str,
    cv: str,
    log_df: pd.DataFrame) -> [xr.Dataset, pd.DataFrame]:

    dir = data_acquisition_dir
    pth = Path(dir) / filename
    ds = xr.open_dataset(pth, engine="h5netcdf", chunks="auto", decode_cf=True)

    cnt_months = ds.dims["valid_time"]
    cnt_lat = ds.dims["latitude"]
    cnt_lon = ds.dims["longitude"]

    if cv == "t":
        var_name = 't2m'
        cnt_values = ds['t2m'].size
    elif cv == "p":
        var_name = 'tp'
        cnt_values = ds['tp'].size

    new_row = {
        "file_name": fil,
        "variable_name": var_name,
        "latitude_count": cnt_lat,
        "longitude_count": cnt_lon,
        "input_month_count": cnt_months,
        "input_values_count": cnt_values,
        "agg_month_count": 12,
        "agg_values_count": cnt_lat * cnt_lon * 12
        }
    log_df = pd.concat([log_df, pd.DataFrame([new_row])], ignore_index=True)
    return ds, log_df

title: 2  Climate Aggregation
text:
2.3 Aggregate Climate


Code
start = datetime.now()

fil = "t_avg_" + str(crp_beg) + "-" + str(crp_end) + "_ERA5.nc"
ds, log_df = fn_import_nc(fil, "t", log_df)
df1 = fn_transform_data(ds, "t", "t2m")

fil = "p_avg_" + str(crp_beg) + "-" + str(crp_end) + "_ERA5.nc"
ds, log_df = fn_import_nc(fil, "p", log_df)
df2 = fn_transform_data(ds, "p", "tp")

fil = "t_avg_" + str(dcp_beg) + "-" + str(dcp_end) + "_ERA5.nc"
ds, log_df = fn_import_nc(fil, "t", log_df)
df3 = fn_transform_data(ds, "t", "t2m")

fil = "p_avg_" + str(dcp_beg) + "-" + str(dcp_end) + "_ERA5.nc"
ds, log_df = fn_import_nc(fil, "p", log_df)
df4 = fn_transform_data(ds, "p", "tp")

wide_df1 = fn_pivot_data(df1, "t")
wide_df2 = fn_pivot_data(df2, "p")
wide_df3 = fn_pivot_data(df3, "t")
wide_df4 = fn_pivot_data(df4, "p")

area_df1 = fn_calculate_area(wide_df1)
area_df2 = fn_calculate_area(wide_df2)
area_df3 = fn_calculate_area(wide_df3)
area_df4 = fn_calculate_area(wide_df4)

end = datetime.now()
print("elapsed time: " + str(end - start))

title: 3  Determinant Calculation
text:
3.1 Initialization
Calculate climate classification determinants using the temperature and precipitation data.
The calculation process is done using R.
It will use packages:
The definitions for the Koppen-Trewartha classification are based on this research: https://scispace.com/pdf/climate-classification-revisited-from-koppen-to-trewartha-40ofumhxzu.pdf

title: 3  Determinant Calculation
text:
3.1.1 Periods


Code
# Climatological Reference Period (crp)
crp_beg <- 1961
crp_end <- 1990

# Decadal Climate Period (dcp)
dcp_beg <- 2015
dcp_end <- 2024




3.1.2 Packages


Code
suppressPackageStartupMessages({



  # Primary packages
  library(tidyverse)
  library(gt)

  # Utililty packages
  library(arrow) # to import and export files in parquet format
  library(patchwork) # to arrange multiple plots into one visualization
  library(RColorBrewer)
  library(scales)
})




3.1.3 Directories


Code
climate_aggregation_dir <- "data/02_climate_aggregation/"
determinant_calculation_dir <- "data/03_determinant_calculation/"

title: 3  Determinant Calculation
3  Determinant Calculation
text:
3.3 Import


Code
fil = paste0("t_agg_", crp_beg, "-", crp_end, ".parquet")
dir <- climate_aggregation_dir 
pth <- paste0(dir, fil)
crp_t_df <- read_parquet(pth) 

fil = paste0("p_agg_", crp_beg, "-", crp_end, ".parquet")
dir <- climate_aggregation_dir 
pth <- paste0(dir, fil)
crp_p_df <- read_parquet(pth) 

fil = paste0("t_agg_", dcp_beg, "-", dcp_end, ".parquet")
dir <- climate_aggregation_dir 
pth <- paste0(dir, fil)
dcp_t_df <- read_parquet(pth) 

fil = paste0("p_agg_", dcp_beg, "-", dcp_end, ".parquet")
dir <- climate_aggregation_dir 
pth <- paste0(dir, fil)
dcp_p_df <- read_parquet(pth)

title: 3  Determinant Calculation
text:
3.4 Temperature Determinants

3.4.1 Function


Code
fn_t_determinants <- function(df) {

  mcols <- paste0("m", 1:12)
  df <- df |>
    filter(lsm > 0) |>
    mutate(
        tavg = (m1 + m2 + m3 + m4 + m5 + m6 + m7 + m8 + m9 + m10 + m11 + m12) / 12,
        tmax = pmax(m1, m2, m3, m4, m5, m6, m7, m8, m9, m10, m11, m12),
        tmin = pmin(m1, m2, m3, m4, m5, m6, m7, m8, m9, m10, m11, m12),
        tg10 = rowSums(across(all_of(mcols), ~ .x >= 10)),
        tg10 = as.integer(tg10)
    )  
  df <- df |>
    select(-m1, -m2, -m3, -m4, -m5, -m6, -m7, -m8, -m9, -m10, -m11, -m12)
  
  return(df)
}




3.4.2 Calculate


Code
crp_t_det_df <- fn_t_determinants(crp_t_df)
dcp_t_det_df <- fn_t_determinants(dcp_t_df)




3.4.3 Determinant: Average Monthly Temperature
Average Monthly Temperature is used to in the aridity threshold calculation.


Code
df1 <- crp_t_det_df |> select(lon, lat, area, tavg) 
df2 <- dcp_t_det_df |> select(lon, lat, area, tavg) 
df <- df1 |>
  left_join(df2, by = c("lon", "lat", "area")) |>
  select(tavg.x, tavg.y, area) |>
  rename(CRP = tavg.x, DCP = tavg.y) |>
  pivot_longer(cols = c(CRP, DCP), names_to  = "cp", values_to = "tavg")

ggplot(df, aes(x = tavg, weight = area, fill = ifelse(tavg <=0, "cold", "warm"))) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 2, boundary = 10, color = "white") + 
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      cold = "#7fb5db",               
      warm = "#c87070"), 
    name = "Category") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(-40, ceiling(max(df$tavg)), by = 10)) +
  labs(
    title = "Average Monthly Temperature",
    subtitle = "Used to Calculate Aridity Threshold",
    x = "°C",
    y = "Weighted Count (by Area)") +
  facet_wrap(~ cp, ncol = 1) +   # one panel for CRP, one for DCP
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  guides(fill = "none")











3.4.4 Determinant: Maximum Month Temperature
Maximum Average Monthly Temperature is used to identify cold regions, using these thresholds:

Ice Cap: < 0 °C
Tundra : 0°C to 10°C



Code
df1 <- crp_t_det_df |> select(lon, lat, area, tmax) 
df2 <- dcp_t_det_df |> select(lon, lat, area, tmax) 
df <- df1 |>
  left_join(df2, by = c("lon", "lat", "area")) |>
  select(tmax.x, tmax.y, area) |>
  rename(CRP = tmax.x, DCP = tmax.y) |>
  pivot_longer(cols = c(CRP, DCP), names_to  = "cp", values_to = "tmax") |>
  mutate(
    category = factor(
      case_when(
        tmax < 0   ~ "Ice Cap",
        tmax < 10  ~ "Tundra",
        tmax >= 10 ~ "Other"
      ),
      levels = c("Ice Cap", "Tundra", "Other")
    )
  )

ggplot(df, aes(x = tmax, weight = area, fill = category)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 2, boundary = 10, color = "white") +  # 10 °C is a bin edge
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("Ice Cap" = "#27679a",   # cool blue-teal
               "Tundra" = "#7fb5db",  # lighter blue-teal
               "Other" = "#d9d9d9"),  # neutral grey
    name = "Category") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(-40, ceiling(max(df$tmax)), by = 10)) +
  labs(
    title = "Maximum Average Monthly Temperature < 10°C",
    subtitle = "Determines Cold Climates",
    x = "°C",
    y = "Weighted Count (by Area)") +
  facet_wrap(~ cp, ncol = 1) +   # one panel for CRP, one for DCP
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  guides(fill = "none")











3.4.5 Determinant: Minimum Month Temperature
Lowest Monthly Average Temperature is used to identify hot regions, using this threshold:

Tropical: > 18°C



Code
df1 <- crp_t_det_df |> select(lon, lat, area, tmin) 
df2 <- dcp_t_det_df |> select(lon, lat, area, tmin) 
df <- df1 |>
  left_join(df2, by = c("lon", "lat", "area")) |>
  select(tmin.x, tmin.y, area) |>
  rename(CRP = tmin.x, DCP = tmin.y) |>
  pivot_longer(cols = c(CRP, DCP), names_to  = "cp", values_to = "tmin")

ggplot(df, aes(x = tmin, weight = area, fill = ifelse(tmin > 18, "hot", "other"))) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 2, boundary = 18, color = "white") +  
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("hot" = "#8b0000",  
               "other" = "#d9d9d9"),  # neutral grey
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = seq(0, max(df$tmin, na.rm = TRUE), by = 6)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Minimum Temperature > 18°C",
    subtitle = "Determines Hot Climates",
    x = "°C",
    y = "Weighted Count (by Area)"
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ cp, ncol = 1) +   # one panel for CRP, one for DCP
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  guides(fill = "none")











3.4.6 Determinant: Months > 10°C
The count of months with average temperature > 10°C is used to identify subtropical, temperate, and boreal climates, using these thresholds:

Subtropical: > 8 or more months
Temperate: 4 to 7 months
Boreal: 1 to 3 months



Code
df1 <- crp_t_det_df |>
  filter(lsm >0 ) |>
  select(lon, lat, area, tg10) 
df2 <- dcp_t_det_df |>
  filter(lsm >0 ) |>
  select(lon, lat, area, tg10) 

df <- df1 |>
  left_join(df2, by = c("lon", "lat", "area")) |>
  select(tg10.x, tg10.y, area) |>
  rename(CRP = tg10.x,
         DCP = tg10.y)

bucket <- function(x) case_when(
  x >= 8 ~ "8-12",
  x >= 4 ~ "4-7",
  x >= 1 ~ "1-3",
  x == 0 ~ "0"
)

df_long <- df %>%
  mutate(
    cat_crp = bucket(CRP),
    cat_dcp = bucket(DCP)
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(cat_crp, cat_dcp),
    names_to = "period",
    values_to = "category"
  ) %>%
  group_by(period, category) %>%
  summarise(total_area = sum(area, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(period = recode(period, cat_crp = "CRP", cat_dcp = "DCP")) %>%
  # compute proportion of grand total
  mutate(prop_area = total_area / sum(total_area))

df_long$category <- factor(df_long$category,
                           levels = c("0", "1-3", "4-7", "8-12"))

ggplot(df_long,
       aes(x = category, y = prop_area, fill = period)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge", width = 0.7) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Dark2") + # or "Dark2", "Paired", etc.
  labs(
    title = "Classification Determinant: Temperature",
    subtitle = "Months with Temperature > 10°C",
    x = "Months per Year",
    y = "Land Area %",
    fill = "Climate\nPeriod",
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)

title: 3  Determinant Calculation
text:
3.5 Precipitation Determinants

3.5.1 Function


Code
fn_p_determinants <- function(df) {

  mcols <- paste0("m", 1:12)
  df <- df |>
    filter(lsm > 0) |>
    mutate(
      ptot = (m1 + m2 + m3 + m4 + m5 + m6 + m7 + m8 + m9 + m10 + m11 + m12),
      pmin = pmin(m1, m2, m3, m4, m5, m6, m7, m8, m9, m10, m11, m12),
      pl60 = rowSums(across(all_of(mcols), ~ .x < 60)),
      pl60 = as.integer(pl60),
      pwnt = case_when(
        lat >= 0 ~ (m1 + m2 + m3 + m10 + m11 + m12),
        lat < 0 ~ (m4 + m5 + m6 + m7 + m8 + m9)
      ),
      psmr = case_when(
        lat >= 0 ~ (m4 + m5 + m6 + m7 + m8 + m9),
        lat < 0 ~  (m1 + m2 + m3 + m10 + m11 + m12)
      )
    )     
  df <- df |>
    select(-m1, -m2, -m3, -m4, -m5, -m6, -m7, -m8, -m9, -m10, -m11, -m12)

  return(df)
}




3.5.2 Calculate


Code
crp_p_det_df <- fn_p_determinants(crp_p_df)
dcp_p_det_df <- fn_p_determinants(dcp_p_df)




3.5.3 Determinant: Seasonal Precipitation
Total Precipitation will be used to identify dry regions. These are the climates identified by this determinant:

Desert (Arid) : < Aridity Threshold / 2
Steppe (Semiarid) : < Aridity Threshold



Code
df1 <- crp_p_det_df |>
  filter(lsm > 0 ) |>
  select(lon, lat, ptot, pwnt, psmr, area) 
df2 <- dcp_p_det_df  |>
  filter(lsm > 0 ) |>
  select(lon, lat, ptot, pwnt, psmr, area) 
dfj <- df1 |>
  left_join(df2, by = c("lon", "lat", "area")) |>
  select(ptot.x, ptot.y, pwnt.x, pwnt.y, psmr.x, psmr.y, area)

df_long <- dfj %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(ptot.x, ptot.y, pwnt.x, pwnt.y, psmr.x, psmr.y),
    names_to = c("metric", "period"),
    names_pattern = "^p(.*)\\.(x|y)$",
    values_to = "precip"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    metric = recode(metric,
                    smr = "Summer",
                    wnt = "Winter",
                    tot = "Total"),
    period = recode(period,
                    x = "CRP",
                    y = "DCP")
  )

# area-weighted mean per metric × period
summ <- df_long %>%
  group_by(metric, period) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_precip = weighted.mean(precip, w = area, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

ggplot(summ, aes(x = period, y = mean_precip, fill = period)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge") +
  facet_wrap(~ metric, ncol = 3) +  
  labs(
    title = "Seasonal Precipitation",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Precipitation (mm)",
    fill = "period"
  ) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Paired") +  # or "Dark2", "Paired", etc.
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    #legend.position = "none",
    #strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),                  # hide tick labels if desired
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank()
  )

title: 3  Determinant Calculation
3  Determinant Calculation
text:
3.7 Save Results


Code
pth = paste0(determinant_calculation_dir, "crp_determinants.parquet")
write_parquet(crp_at_df, pth)

pth = paste0(determinant_calculation_dir, "dcp_determinants.parquet")
write_parquet(dcp_at_df, pth)

title: 3  Determinant Calculation
text:
3.8 Build Summary Table


Code
df1 <- fn_weighted_average(crp_at_df)
df2 <- fn_weighted_average(dcp_at_df)

df_wgtavg <- df1 |> 
  full_join(df2, by = "metric") |> 
  rename(
    crp_metric = wgtavg.x,
    dcp_metric = wgtavg.y) |> 
  mutate(metric_chg = dcp_metric - crp_metric) |> 
  mutate(pct_chg = metric_chg / crp_metric) |> 
  mutate(
    metric = recode(metric,
      "tavg" = "Temperature: Avg",
      "tmax" = "Temperature: Max",
      "tmin" = "Temperature: Min",
      "tg10" = "Temperature: Months > 10°C",
      "ptot" = "Precipitation: Total",
      "pmin" = "Precipitation: Min",
      "pl60" = "Precipitation: Months < 60mm",
      "pwnt" = "Precipitation: Winter",
      "psmr" = "Precipitation: Summer",
      "athr" = "Aridity Threshold"
    )
  )

title: 3  Determinant Calculation
text:
3.9 Display Summary Table


Code
rows_to_style <- which(
  df_wgtavg[["pct_chg"]] > 0.05)

gt_tbl <- 
  df_wgtavg |>
    gt(rowname_col = "metric") |>
    tab_header(
      title = "Climate Classification Determinants Summary",
      subtitle = "Area-Weighted Averages"
    ) |>
    cols_label(
      crp_metric = "CRP",
      dcp_metric = "DCP",
      metric_chg = "Change",
      pct_chg = "% Change",
    ) |>
    fmt_number(
      columns = c(crp_metric, dcp_metric, metric_chg, pct_chg),
      decimals = 2
    ) |>
    tab_style(
      style = cell_fill(color = "#e6f0fa"),          # highlight color
      locations = cells_body(columns = "pct_chg", rows = rows_to_style)
    ) |>
    tab_style(
      style = cell_text(align = "left"),   # left-justify
      locations = cells_stub()
    )


gt_tbl

title: 4  Climate Classification
text:
4.1 Periods
Use the classification determinants to calculate the climate type for each grid cell, using the Köppen-Trewartha classification system.
Code
# Climatological Reference Period (crp)
crp_beg <- 1961
crp_end <- 1990
crp_lbl <-  paste0("CRP ",crp_beg,"-",crp_end)

# Decadal Climate Period (dcp)
dcp_beg <- 2015
dcp_end <- 2024
dcp_lbl <-  paste0("DCP ",dcp_beg,"-",dcp_end)

title: 4  Climate Classification
text:
4.2 Packages


Code
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(tidyverse)
  library(sf)
  library(arrow)
  library(gt)
  library(units)
  library(patchwork)
})

pth <- paste0(dir_rf,"ktc_group_palette.csv")

ktc_grp_palette_str <- setNames(
  ktc_grp_palette_df$hex,
  ktc_grp_palette_df$grp
)

pth <- paste0(dir_rf,"ktc_type_palette.csv")
ktc_typ_palette_df <- read_csv(pth, show_col_types = FALSE)
ktc_typ_levels <- ktc_typ_palette_df$typ
ktc_typ_palette_str <- setNames(
  ktc_typ_palette_df$hex,
  ktc_typ_palette_df$typ
)

title: 5  Change Analysis
text:
5.1 Periods
Code
# Climatological Reference Period
crp = "crp"
crp_beg <- 1961
crp_end <- 1990
crp_lbl <- paste("CRP ", crp_beg, "-", crp_end, sep = "")
# Recent Climate Period
dcp = "dcp"
dcp_beg <- 2015
dcp_end <- 2024
dcp_lbl <- paste("DCP ", dcp_beg, "-", dcp_end, sep = "")

title: 5  Change Analysis
text:
5.2 Packages


Code
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(tidyverse)
  library(sf)
  library(arrow)
  library(units)
  library(gt)
  library(patchwork)
})

title: 5  Change Analysis
text:
5.3 Directories


Code
dir_rf <- "data/00_reference/"
climate_classification_dir <- "data/04_climate_classification/"

title: 5  Change Analysis
text:
5.4 Palette


Code
pth <- paste0(dir_rf,"ktc_group_palette.csv")
palette_df<- read_csv(pth, show_col_types = FALSE)
grp_levels <- palette_df$grp
grp_palette <- setNames(
  palette_df$hex,
  palette_df$grp
)

title: 5  Change Analysis
text:
5.5 Projections


Code
rbn_prj <- "ESRI:54030"

title: 5  Change Analysis
text:
5.6 Coastline


Code
dir <- dir_rf
fil <- "global-coastline.gpkg"
pth <- paste(dir, fil, sep = "")
coastline_sf <- st_read(pth, quiet=TRUE)

title: 5  Change Analysis
text:
5.7 Import


Code
pth <- paste(climate_classification_dir, "ktc_grp_classification.gpkg", sep = "")
import_sf <- st_read(pth, quiet=TRUE)

import_sf <- import_sf |>  
  mutate(cgrp = factor(cgrp, levels = grp_levels))
import_sf <- import_sf |>  
  mutate(rgrp = factor(rgrp, levels = grp_levels))
import_df <- import_sf |> 
  st_drop_geometry() 

# remove rows where rgrp and cgrp are the same
shifts_sf <- import_sf |> filter(cgrp != rgrp)
shifts_df <- import_df |> filter(cgrp != rgrp)

title: 5  Change Analysis
5  Change Analysis
text:
5.9 Analysis

5.9.1 Summary

5.9.1.1 Summary Table


Code
# summmarize crp group mkm and pct
df1 <- import_df |>
  summarize(
    mkm_c = sum(mkm),
    pct_c = sum(pct),
    .by = c(cgrp)) |>
  rename(cg = cgrp)

# summmarize dcp group mkm and pct
df2 <- import_df |>
  summarize(
    mkm_r = sum(mkm),
    pct_r = sum(pct),
    .by = c(rgrp)) |>
  rename(cg = rgrp)

# join crp and dcp and calculate difference
join_df <- df1 |> 
  inner_join(df2, by = "cg") |>
  mutate(mkm_d = mkm_r - mkm_c) |>
  mutate(pct_d = pct_r - pct_c)

# shifts from other groups
df3 <- shifts_df |>
  summarize(
    mkm_g = sum(mkm),
    pct_g = sum(pct),
    .by = c(rgrp)) |>
  rename(cg = rgrp)

# join tables
join_df <- join_df  |> 
  inner_join(df3, by = "cg")

# shifts to other groups
df4 <- shifts_df |>
  summarize(
    mkm_l = sum(mkm) * -1,
    pct_l = sum(pct) * -1,
    .by = c(cgrp)) |>
  rename(cg = cgrp)

# join tables
join_df <- join_df  |> 
    inner_join(df4, by = "cg")
    
# select columns for table to display    
tbl_df <- join_df  |> 
    select(cg,
      mkm_c, mkm_g, mkm_l, mkm_r, mkm_d,
      pct_c, pct_g, pct_l, pct_r, pct_d
    ) |> 
    mutate(across(c(pct_c, pct_g, pct_l, pct_r, pct_d), ~ .x / 100))

# create gt table
t1 <- fn_build_summary_table(tbl_df)




5.9.1.2 Summary Map


Code
# Build dcp map
plot_data <- shifts_sf |>
  select(rgrp) |>
  rename(grp = rgrp)
p1 <- fn_build_global_map (plot_data, grp_palette, "dcp")

# build crp map
plot_data <- shifts_sf |>
  select(cgrp) |>
  rename(grp = cgrp)
p2 <- fn_build_global_map (plot_data, grp_palette, "crp")

5.9.2.2 Detail Analysis


Code
fn_detail_analysis <- function(shifts_df, shifts_sf, grp_palette, g) {

  tbl_df <- shifts_df |> 
  filter(rgrp == g) |>
  mutate(pct = pct / 100) |>
  arrange(rgrp)
  t1 <- fn_build_detail_table(tbl_df, paste("New Group ", g))

  g_new_sf <- shifts_sf |> 
  filter(rgrp == g) |>
  select(rgrp) |>
  rename(grp = rgrp)

  p1 <- fn_build_global_map(g_new_sf, grp_palette, "dcp")

  g_old_sf <- shifts_sf |> 
  filter(rgrp == g) |>
  select(cgrp) |>
  rename(grp = cgrp)

  p2 <- fn_build_global_map(g_old_sf, grp_palette, "crp")

 return(list(t1 = t1, p1 = p1, p2 = p2))

}




Code
results = fn_detail_analysis(shifts_df, shifts_sf, grp_palette, "A")
results$t1
